messages.subject: [R-jobs] Work From Home Statistical Analyst Opening
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Work From Home Statistical Analyst Opening
messages.body_plain:
We are currently seeking a Statistical Analyst to work for a large Pharmaceutical company in the US. This position is a Work From Home opportunity. If you meet the requirements below please send your resume to Christina.Kinchen at InventivHealth.com or call 407-548-0670. 

REQUIRED: 
*        The ideal candidate will have strong R programming skills and 6 months, or more, work placement/intern/post-graduate work in an epidemiological research environment (regulatory, pharmaceutical or CRO) 
*        Experience working in data mining or working with big data 
*        Well-developed planning, observation, analytical and problem-solving skills 
*        Experience designing, implementing and writing statistical analysis plans 
*        The ability to articulate complex statistical/technical issues to non-experts 
*        Project management, time management and technical capabilities 
*        The ideal candidate will have Pharmaceutical / CRO experience in order to have a good understanding of the processes associated with the conduct of clinical trials and operations
*        Strong written and verbal communication skills, this includes presentations skills (English) 
*        Must be capable of building strong relationships with colleagues and service providers 
*        A knowledge of working across international boundaries and cultures Comfortable with ambiguity and working in a complex matrix environment.
*       This position Can be virtual but business is based in the North East.  
*       Some travel to business sites in East coast locations. 
 
Job Description:  
*        Monitor the predictive power of the existing algorithms by reviewing, statistically validating the existing statistical code and updating as needed, 
*        Partner with business, data, technology, statistical and other SMEs to develop hypothesizes regarding what makes an Investigator Site "Healthy", 
*        Will partner with data/system SMEs to develop a robust understanding of available data attributes and limitations, 
*        Be responsible for data analytics / data science on massive sets of complex and, sometimes, unstructured data to prepare for statistical analysis, 
*        Partner with Statistician Lead to leverage statistical methodologies, techniques and analytics to deliver deep insights into "Site Health" i.e. epidemiology/correlations to relevant outcomes, 
*        Writing new or modifying existing statistical code, currently using R as programming language, 
*        Collaborate with Statistician Lead to interpret, write-up and present results of conducted analyses, 
*        Maintain and update all relevant statistical documentation. 
*        Will be comfortable interacting at all levels of technology teams (project managers, database designer, information architecture, multilayer off-shore support centers, etc.). 
*        Manage projects to ensure they are delivered successfully and in a timely manner.
 

If this sounds like the right fit for your background please submit your resume to Christina Kinchen: Christina.Kinchen at InventivHealth.com or call 407-548-0670.
We offer a 1500.00 Referral fee - Ask for details


Christina Kinchen
Senior Recruitment Specialist
inVentiv Health Clinical
T 800-684-9921 ext 80670 or +1 407-548-0670
Christina.Kinchen at inventivhealth.com

inVentiv Health Clinical, LLC, an inVentiv Health, Inc. company, is an Equal Opportunity Employer, M/F/D/V that values the strength that diversity brings to the workplace.

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Work From Home Lead Statistician Opening
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Work From Home Lead Statistician Opening
messages.body_plain:
We are currently seeking a Lead Statistician to work for a large Pharmaceutical company in the US. This position is a Work From Home opportunity. If you meet the requirements below please send your resume to Christina.Kinchen at InventivHealth.com or call 407-548-0670. 
A brief description is as follows:

Title of Position: Telecommuting LEAD STATISTICIAN
REQUIRED: 
. The ideal candidate will have experience with R and  1-2 years, or more, work placement/post-graduate work in an epidemiological research environment (regulatory, pharmaceutical or CRO) 
. Experience working in data mining or working with big data 
. Well-developed planning, observation, analytical and problem-solving skills 
. Experience designing, implementing and writing statistical analysis plans 
. The ability to articulate complex statistical/technical issues to non-experts 
. Project management, time management and technical capabilities 
. The ideal candidate will have Pharmaceutical / CRO experience in order to have a good understanding of the processes associated with the conduct of clinical trials and operations
. This position Can be virtual but business is based in the North East.  
. Some travel to business sites in East coast locations.
?
Other: 
. Strong written and verbal communication skills, this includes presentations skills (English) 
. Must be capable of building strong relationships with colleagues and service providers 
. A knowledge of working across international boundaries and cultures Comfortable with ambiguity and working in a complex matrix environment
?
Job Description:? 
The Driving Site Health Statisticians Lead will: 
. Partner with business, data, technology, statistical and other colleagues/SMEs to develop hypothesizes regarding what makes an Investigator Site "Healthy", 
. Review and analyze hypothesis, questions or problems in order to deliver optimal plans/solutions to meet the needs, 
. Will partner with data/system SMEs to develop a robust understanding of available data attributes and limitations, 
. Partner with the Statistician Analyst to ensure data is cleaned, prepared and ready for statistical analysis, 
. Leverage statistical methodologies, techniques and analytics to deliver deep insights into "Site Health" i.e. epidemiology/correlations to relevant outcomes, 
. Develop cutting edge predictive modeling tools that enable CTSC to predict lower 'Site Health' . Partner with the Statistician Analyst to ensure effective delivery of repeatable statistical models programmed in R 
. Developing, implementing and, as needed, writing statistical analysis plans, 
. Be comfortable presenting and translating statistical concepts in simple terms to a broad range of audiences and stakeholders (external and internal), including senior executives 
. Manage projects to ensure they are delivered successfully and in a timely manner.

If this sounds like the right fit for your background please submit your resume to Christina Kinchen: Christina.Kinchen at InventivHealth.com or call 407-548-0670
We offer a 1500.00 Referral fee - Ask for details

inVentiv Health Clinical, LLC, an inVentiv Health, Inc. company, is an Equal Opportunity Employer, M/F/D/V that values the strength that diversity brings to the workplace.



Christina Kinchen
Senior Recruitment Specialist
inVentiv Health Clinical
T 800-684-9921 ext 80670 or +1 407-548-0670
Christina.Kinchen at inventivhealth.com

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Data Scientist – Marketing Science – Los Angeles
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Data Scientist – Marketing Science – Los Angeles
messages.body_plain:
Data Scientist ? Marketing Science ? Los Angeles

Ipsos ? a global market research firm ? has an opening for a Data Scientist.? We seek a capable and creative programmer with unrelenting attention to detail, an ability to work well with a small team (including both programmers and non-programmers), and a strong interest in advanced analytic techniques.? Fluency in R and/or Python is required.

Responsibilities:
? Contribute to large-scale data and statistical modeling efforts.
? Develop new data science products by translating conceptual blueprints into polished applications.
? Maintain and enhance large codebases underlying existing key applications.
? Perform other marketing science analyses for clients as required.

Required Skills:
The ideal candidate has the following skills and experience:
Required
? Proficiency in advanced R and/or Python programming, including the ability to debug and streamline very large functions.
Preferred
? Experience in parallelizing processes.
? Experience integrating R and/or Python with each other and C\C++.
? Large dataset manipulation.
? Working knowledge of SQL.? Further experience with database administration considered a plus. 
? Experience creating and deploying web applications.
? Familiarity with web scraping tools and techniques.
? Experience with JavaScript, esp. visualization libraries.
? Familiarity with particular analytic tools including Monte Carlo simulation, machine learning, genetic algorithms, data fusion, predictive models, network analysis, system dynamics, and/or agent-based models.
? Advanced degree (M.S., Ph.D.) preferred but by no means required.

If interested apply at http://bit.ly/1rFm2EU or reply with a resume.

Regards,

Drew

Andrew Christianson
Research Manager, Ipsos Science Centre

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Research Scientist positions at University of Washington
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Research Scientist positions at University of Washington
messages.body_plain:
Multiple Research Scientist positions are available at the University of Washington (UW) Center for Data Science (http://cwds.uw.edu) located at the UW Tacoma campus for outstanding researchers to conduct applied social good oriented multidisciplinary research in areas including:

Bioinformatics (with Prof. Ka Yee Yeung), Data Science (Prof. Senjuti Basu Roy), Geospatial Informatics & Mobile Computing (Prof. Mohamed Ali and Prof. Ling Ding), Healthcare Analytics (Prof. Ankur Teredesai), and Machine Learning & Computational Intelligence (Prof. Martine De Cock).  

The Research Scientist will work with this collaborative team on multiyear funded projects with access to real-world datasets, well-known industry partners, and a track-record of deployed applications. The team comprises of faculty and postdocs publishing at high impact venues, and highly-talented engineers with significant industry experience leading to innovate demonstrable solutions. Our culture encourages recent Masters and Ph.D.s to develop an interest in growing both their engineering skills to handle large datasets and bring an entrepreneurial spirit to work on societal data science.

The current projects involve:
1.	The underlying theme of our computational biology projects is to develop machine learning methods for the mining of big ?omics? data in biology. Our overarching goal is to develop personalized medicine that uses genetic information to tailor therapy for individual patients.  Collaboration with an interdisciplinary team (e.g. clinicians and biologists) is an essential component in our projects.
2.	Health Care Analytics effort focuses on advancing the state of the art through development of advanced algorithms as well as deployment of real world tools that can be applied effectively across a number of users scenarios. 
3.	A mobile ad-hoc network is an infrastructure-less network consisting of wireless-capable mobile devices moving freely and independently from other wireless devices in the same network. Due to the low node density and unstable network topology in this type of networks, end-to-end routing is impossible. Mobility-assisted data access technique becomes a practical choice. This project is to study how to achieve efficient data dissemination in mobile ad-hoc networks. Candidates with background of game theory, networking, algorithm, and GIS are welcome. 
4.	The Healthcare Big Data Stack project leverages open-source technologies for solving challenging issues in database management and data mining for varied and complex healthcare data including claims data, clinical data, patient encounter data as well as us government datasets to enable machine learning researchers to rapidly prototype healthcare applications that help save costs and provide robust predictive analytics for chronic diseases.


Qualifications:
?	A recent Masters or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, specialized in the above areas, or any physical sciences with a strong background in mathematics and computing. 
?	Demonstrated expertise in solving challenging problems in one or more of the above areas.
?	Preference given to candidates with interest in applying their skills in multidisciplinary application domains.
?	Demonstrated expertise in technical writing as evidenced by publication record.
?	Strong interpersonal communication skills and ability to interact with peers as well as students.
?	Ability and interest to conduct independent yet collaborative research.

Responsibilities
?	conducting innovative research.
?	managing project goals and driving outcomes.
?	writing high-quality research papers.
?	participating in writing and applying for grants.
?	mentoring and teaching undergraduate and graduate students.

The Institute of Technology which is the academic home for the Center for Data Science at UW Tacoma has a well-established computer science, computer engineering and Information Technology curriculum and provides excellent small-classroom environment and access to expert teaching focused faculty as mentors to new teachers to help develop and grow teaching skills.

Please send a CV, short research statement, and list of references to kayee at uw.edu.
